name: mechanize (Stateful HTML Browser in Python)

auth.notes: HTTP browser; site auth handled via forms

agent_readiness.notes: Stateful programmatic web browser for HTTP-based scraping. import mechanize. br = mechanize.Browser(). br.set_handle_robots(False). br.open('https://example.com'). br.select_form(nr=0). br['username'] = 'user'. br['password'] = 'pass'. br.submit(). br.links() → all links. br.follow_link(url='https://...') → follow link. br.back() → navigate back. br.reload(). br.response().read(). br.title(). br.set_handle_redirect(True). Cookies: br.set_cookiejar(cookielib.CookieJar()). HTTP auth: br.add_password('url', 'user', 'pass'). No JavaScript — pure HTTP only. Like Perl's WWW::Mechanize. Forms: mechanize.HTMLForm.

gotchas: No JavaScript support — fails on any JS-rendered content
Older API patterns — not Pythonic by modern standards
For any modern site, use Playwright or Selenium instead
best_when: Legacy form submission automation on non-JS sites
Simple HTTP-based scraping with cookie management
When requests-html is too heavy and plain requests lacks form support
Automating old web apps that don't use JavaScript
avoid_when: Any JavaScript-rendered content (use Playwright or Selenium)
New projects — use httpx + BeautifulSoup4 instead
summary: Mechanize is a stateful programmatic HTTP browser for Python. br = mechanize.Browser(); br.open(url); br.select_form(nr=0); br['field'] = 'value'; br.submit(). Cookie handling, redirects, link following. BSD-3-Clause. No JavaScript support — purely HTTP. Mostly stalled. Best for old form-based site automation without JS. For modern sites use Playwright. For scraping use httpx + BeautifulSoup4 or Scrapy.
